cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The user provided a passage about Yves Gomes and then asked which description about him is true, with four options. First, I need to read the passage carefully and then check each option against the information given. From the passage: Yves Gomes is a university student in Maryland who may have to leave the country. He was brought to the US by his parents when he was 14 months old. His parents were deported more than a year ago. He wants to continue his education and go to medical school. He is fighting deportation to India, which he left with his parents at 14 months. The DREAMAct is mentioned as pending legislation that could help undocumented immigrants who were brought to the US before 16 to become legal residents if they complete college or join the military. Yves hopes the DREAMAct passes so he can finish school and become a citizen. Now, let's look at the options. Option A: He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. The passage says he was brought to the US at 14 months old. It doesn't explicitly state where he was born. However, since he was deported to India, it's possible he was born in the US, but the passage doesn't say. The passage says he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old, which might imply he was born in India and they immigrated to the US. Wait, but the phrasing is a bit confusing. The passage says: "he is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old." So he left India at 14 months,
